rationale: The 2006 paper is cited only in grouped references to support a broad background claim that consumers want their actions to reflect good moral character. The citing article does not discuss the licensing effect findings, mechanism, or boundary conditions from Khan and Dhar (2006), nor does it build specific hypotheses or analyses on that study. Because the citation serves as general prior-literature support rather than a distinct conceptual or empirical dependency, its role is best classified as a passing mention.
